texts.full_text: he third largest island of the Novaya Zemlya archipelago, lying north of Russia's mainland. It lies in the Barents Sea to the west of the much larger Yuzhny Island. The area of the Mezhdusharsky Island is . See also List Mezgduskary Strait The island was first mapped by Captain Ferdinand von Heinegg in 1876 during his surveying expedition around Novaya Zemlya. It has been inhabited sporadically throughout history, with the last permanent settlement being established there until Soviet times. Geography and Features Mezhdusharsky Island is characterized primarily by its rugged terrain featuring a mix of mountainsous cliffs along coastlines interspersed among sandy beaches in some areas. The island's elevation varies from sea level up to 400 meters above the surrounding waters, with volcanic rocks scattered across much landscape. The climate on Mezhdusharsky Island is harsh and extreme; it falls under a polar marine environment due its location within Arctic Circle boundaries near Russia’s northernmost region of Murmansk Oblast. Winters are long-drawn out periods marked by frigid temperatures, while summers offer brief but pleasantly short days with relatively milder conditions. Economic Activities Historically Mezhdusharsky Island has been used for commercial fishing and hunting due to its vast stretches along the Barents Sea rich in marine life. The island's remote location also makes it an unlikely site of significant human habitation, though a few seasonal research expeditions do visit periodically. The region is home primarily only around 200 reindeer herders during summer months who utilize available natural resources for sustenance and shelt

texts.full_text: Alfred Nobel. The winners of this year's prizes will be announced daily from Monday, October 3 through October 10. Here are five things to know about the prizes and their creator. Posthumous awards Since 1974, the statutes of the Nobel Foundation stipulate that the prize may not be given posthumously. But a person may be awarded if she or he dies between the time of the announcement in October and the formal prize ceremony in December. Close Before the change, only two people had won a Nobel posthumously. One was Dag Hammarskjold, the Swedish secretary general of the United Nations who died in a plane crash in 1961 but was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize later the same year. texts.full_text: sed on the temperature related factors, such as heat wave, cold wave, and extreme temperature. Some studies suggested that cold temperature increased risks for stroke \[[@b5-medscimonit-21-3600],[@b6-medscimonit-21-3600]\] while others indicated that high temperature was positively correlated with stroke attack \[[@b7-medscimonit-21-3600]--[@b9-medscimonit-21-3600]\]. Temperature fluctuation has a closer relationship with stroke attack than the absolute temperature value \[[@b10-medscimonit-21-36000]\]. Temperature fluctuation, or the variability in temperature, has been shown to exert a more significant impact on the incidence of stroke, possibly due to the body's stress response to rapid temperature changes, which may lead to changes in blood pressure, blood viscosity, and the release of stress hormones, all of which are potential stroke triggers \[[@b11-medscimonit-21-3600],[@b12-medscimonit-21-3600]\]. Moreover, the relationship between stroke and temperature fluctuation may also be influenced by the baseline climate of the region, the adaptation of the population, and the healthcare infrastructure available. For example, populations in regions with more consistent climate may be more susceptible to temperature fluctuations, whereas populations in regions with more extreme and variable climates may have developed physiological and behavioral adaptations to mitigate the adverse effects of temperature changes. Additionally, the impact of temperature fluctuations may be more pronounced in areas with less developed healthcare systems, where early detection and timely treatment of stroke may be less accessible \[[@b13-medscimonit-21-3600]\]. Therefore, understanding the impact of temperature fluctuation on stroke, and the underlying mechanisms, is crucial for the development of effective public

texts.full_text: by consistently low acute stress scores across all assessment points, suggesting these patients experienced minimal distress related to their breast cancer diagnosis throughout follow-up. Psychological predictors of these trajectories were identified through regression analysis. Predictive factors included baseline levels of depressive symptoms and coping strategies such as avoidance behavior at the time of diagnosis (T0). Patients with higher initial depression scores or those who tended to engage in more avoidant coping mechanisms reported a greater likelihood of following either the high or moderate acute stress trajectory. The findings from this study contribute valuable insights into how different breast cancer patients experience and cope with their illness over time. Understanding these patterns can help healthcare providers tailor psychological interventions specifically aimed at reducing distress for those most vulnerable to prolonged periods of heightened emotional strain throughout treatment and follow-up care. Future research should continue to explore the long-term implications of acute stress trajectories on overall quality of life, as well as identify additional predictors such as personality traits or social support networks that may contribute to these distinct pathways. Furthermore, examining potential mediators between psychological interventions and changes in acute stress over time could provide evidence for effective strategies aimed at mitigating the negative impact of breast cancer diagnosis and treatment on mental health outcomes. In conclusion, this study has highlighted the importance of recognizing individual differences in coping mechanisms and emotional responses to a life-altering event such as a non-metastatic breast cancer diagnosis. texts.full_text: respecting "consumer" autonomy. The unfortunately vast history of paternalism in both medicine and clinical research has resulted in perpetually increasing respect for patient autonomy and free choice in Western health care systems. Beginning with the negative right to informed consent, the principle of respect for autonomy has for many patients evolved into a positive right to request treatments and expect accommodation. This evolution of patient autonomy has mirrored a more general social attitude of market liberalism where increasing numbers of patients have come to embody the role of the "consumer." This paper evaluates the implications of market liberalism and the role of the "consumer" within the health care system. This emergency con trol unit estimates an emergency event using sensor signals input from various sensors such as a radar sensor and a camera, and switches the target of a current vehicle operation to an emergency target vehicle when an emergency event is estimated. A vehicle speed setting unit sets the travel speed to a prescribed travel speed. However, this vehicle speed management has not been designed to be easily understood by a user. The user often has difficulty in easily understanding what the vehicle speed management intends. In particular, the user cannot understand a change of the travel speed according to a driving situation in real time. To solve the above problem, a vehicle speed management device according to the invention disclosed in Patent Literature 2 sets a travel speed threshold value corresponding to each type of road. Eu quero que quando eu passe o mouse em cima da li, o span mude. Pois hoje, o span está mudando só quando passo o mouse em cima do span. A: Você foi pelo caminho certo, mas colocou o :hover no lugar errado. texts.full_text: using System.Linq; using System.Text; using System.Threading.Tasks; using MongoDB.Bson.Serialization; using MongoDB.Bson.Serialization.Serializers; namespace MongoDB.Bson.Serialization.Serializers { /// <summary> /// Wraps a serializer and projects using a function. /// </summary> /// <typeparam name="TFrom">The type of from.</typeparam> /// <typeparam name="TTo">The type of to.</typeparam> public class ProjectingDeserializer<TFrom, TTo> : SerializerBase<TTo> { // private fields private readonly IBsonSerializer<TFrom> _fromSerializer; private readonly Func<TFrom, TTo> _projector; // constructors /// <summary> /// Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="ProjectingDeserializer{TFrom, TTo}"/> class. /// </summary> /// <param name="fromSerializer">From serializer.</param> /// <param name="projector">The projector.</param> public ProjectingDeserializer(IBsonSerializer<TFrom> fromSerializer, Func<TFrom, TTo> projector) { _fromSerializer = fromSerializer; _projector = projector; } /// <summary> /// Deserializes a value. /// </summary> /// <param name="context">The deserialization context.</param> /// <param name="args">The deserialization args.</param> /// <returns>A deserialized value.</returns> public override TTo Deserialize(BsonDeserializationContext context, BsonDeserializationArgs args) { var from = _fromSerializer.Deserialize(context); return _projector(from); } }

Banks have long been bound by regulations that have worked and served them efficiently in the past, but are now restricting them from new and innovative implementations. Aki Ranin, commercial director at Tigerspike, which is a global digital products company, says that Millennials are used to what’s free – they do not have a lot of money, and evenif they did, they prefer the convenience fintech offers over traditional banking methods. This inclination toward tech-driven financial solutions stems from their extensive use of technology in other aspects of life. The landscape is shifting as millennials' expectations are shaping how businesses operate across industries. They expect fast, easy access to services and information through various platforms. Businesses that don't meet these criteria will inevitably find themselves losing a significant portion of the market share—something many banks have already started experiencing due to fintech competition. Millennials’ embrace of technology isn't just about personal preference; it's rooted in their priorities for simplicity, accessibility, and real-time interaction. For instance, they may prefer mobile banking apps that allow them to pay bills with a swipe rather than scheduling time for visits or transactions at physical bank branches. But while banks are slow to adapt to this reality due to legacy systems, fintech startups thrive on innovation. They leverage the latest technologies such as AI and blockchain which make financial services more transparent, secure, and efficient – something that resonates well with millennials who value instant gratification and operational efficiency in every sphere of their life. The evolution towards a cashless society has been further accelerated by digital wallet solutions like PayPal or Apple Pay. texts.full_text: e opened or closed by a valve plate, and a seat associated with the port, in which the turning of a shaft in the one direction produces a movement of the valve plate from its open position to its closed position, and thereafter a pressing movement of the valve plate against the seat, and in which a turning of the shaft in the other direction produces movement of the valve plate in the reverse direction. In operating mechanisms of this kind (cf., for example, the pendulum valve of GB-PS No. 1,050,435) it is important that the valve plate perform no additional lateral movements during the movement by which it is pressed perpendicularly against the valve seat, so that the often very delicate sealing surfaces will not exercise a rubbing action against one another. It is furthermore imporant that the valve plate be located in a position as close to the seat as possible during its pressing movement, to avoid a long distance of travel between the valve plate and the seat during the period of closing of the valve. To achieve these goals, according to the invention, the operating mechanism is characterized in that the shaft can be turned around by means of an actuating device in such a way that the valve plate is pressed against the seat in the one direction and turned around at least partly in the other direction. This allows the valve plate to move smoothly towards the seat for closing while minimizing wear on the sealing surfaces, as well as reducing the distance required for the valve plate to travel once it has been pressed against the seat during the closing process. Now, let's take it a step further and establish a community equipment and supplies pool to help our less financially fortunate members. Many of our fellow photography enthusiasts, especially in regions like the former Soviet Union, have a passion for the craft but lack the necessary equipment. We can change that. texts.full_text: and this is intentional behavior. Here's why: When joining a multicast group, the socket needs to specify the IP address and port number of the group's sender or any other member that can be used for communication with the group. The port number is not fixed for a multicast group, as multiple members may be using the same port number. Therefore, the `SocketAddress` object is required to specify the IP address and port number of the group's sender or any other member. Even if the port number does not matter for a specific multicast group, the `joinGroup()` method still needs to know the IP address of the group's sender or any other member that can be used for communication with the group.
